title: CHIEF MINISTER ATTENDS DISTRIBUTION OF OXYGEN CONCENTRATORS DONATED BY FORMOSA FOUNDATION
content:
102/2026-2027

Aizawl, 23 May 2026 :Chief Minister Shri Lalduhoma today attended as Chief Guest the distribution programme of 500 oxygen concentrators donated by Formosa Foundation through the Central Young Mizo Association (CYMA). The programme was held at Vanapa Hall, Aizawl. Lt. Gen. Vikas Lakhera, Director General of Assam Rifles, attended the function as Guest of Honour.

Addressing the gathering, the Chief Minister said that Formosa Foundation has consistently extended valuable support to Mizoram and its people over the years and has always been a source of blessing for the State. He expressed deep appreciation for the Foundation’s continued generosity.

He recalled that Formosa Foundation, in collaboration with CYMA, had earlier donated ambulances to 13 Group YMAs, one vehicle each to MTP and YLA Sub-Headquarters, 500 sets of disaster relief blankets and 190 sets of kitchen utensils to CYMA. The Foundation had also provided Bolero Campers to nine YMA Sub-Headquarters for anti-drug initiatives, school buses to two schools, and numerous donations to orphanages, hospitals and churches. The Chief Minister particularly acknowledged the Foundation’s immense contribution during the COVID-19 pandemic.

The Chief Minister also expressed gratitude to Lt. Gen. Vikas Lakhera and the Assam Rifles for their continued service to Mizoram and for assisting in the transportation and distribution of the oxygen concentrators.

Speaking on the occasion, Lt. Gen. Vikas Lakhera said his own family had personally experienced the importance of oxygen concentrators, and expressed confidence that the donated units would greatly benefit those in need. He thanked Formosa Foundation for its humanitarian assistance to the people.

Shri Jonathan Lalremruata, Chairman of Formosa Foundation, also addressed the gathering. He stated that the Foundation remains committed to assisting those in need and contributing in every possible way for Mizoram. He expressed hope that the oxygen concentrators would be put to good use for patients requiring medical support, and reaffirmed the Foundation’s desire to continue working together with the Government and NGOs for the welfare of the State.

The programme was chaired by Prof. Lalnuntluanga, Vice President of CYMA. Father Jerome Vungbiakdika led the invocation, while Prof. Malsawmliana, General Secretary of CYMA, presented a report. Shri R. Lalngheta, President of CYMA, also delivered a speech.

The 500 oxygen concentrators distributed on this occasion were allocated not only to selected YMA branches within Mizoram, but also to branches in Assam, Tripura and Manipur, as well as certain YLA and MTP branches. Zuangtui Branch YMA donated its allotted units to Behliangchhip Branch in Tripura.

Attachment

title: FORMOSA FOUNDATION BUL TUMA OXYGEN CONCENTRATOR SEMNA CHIEF MINISTER-IN HMANPUI
content:
684/2026-2024

Aizawl the 23rd May 2026 :Vawiin khan Formosa Foundation ten Central YMA kaltlanga Branch YMA te hnena Oxygen Concentrator 500  hlanna inkhawm, Vanapa Hall-a an buatsaih chu Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chuan khuallian niin a telpui a, Lt. Gen. Vikas Lakhera, DG, Assam Rifles chu khualzahawm a ni.

Thusawiin Chief Minister chuan, Formosa Foundation hian kan ram leh mipuite tan thahnemngai takin hma an lo la tawh thîn a. Malsawmna thlentu an ni fo thin. Vawiinah an han luang chhuak leh thei hi kan lawm em em a ni.

Tlem lai khan Central YMA nena tangkawpin Group YMA 13 te hnenah Ambulance an pe a, MTP leh YLA Sub Hqr pakhat ve ve an pe a. Central YMA hnenah vek hian rikrum thila kan mamawh Puan In 500 leh Choka mamawh set 190 a lo pe tawh a. Ruihhlo dona atan YMA Sub Hqr 9 te chu Bolero Camper a pe a. School pahnih hnenah Bus a pe a, hnuchham inah te, damdawiinah te, kohhranah te thilpek an pe hnem tawh hle. Covid19 hripui len laia an hnathawhte phei kha chu a tam tham hle a, kan tangkaipui em em a ni, a ti.

Chief Minister hian Lt. Gen Vikas Lakhera leh Assam Rifles ten Mizoram tana hna an thawhna kawngah te, tun tuma semchhuah oxygen concentrator phurh kualna lama an lo tanpui avangtein lawmthu a sawi bawk.

Lt. Gen. Vikas Lakhera pawhin thusawiin, kan chhungkua hian oxygen concentrator hlutzia hi a takin kan lo tawng ve tawh a. Heng concentrator, vawiina semchhuahte pawh hi a mamawhtute tan a hlu dawn tih ka hria a. Mipuite ban phaka hetianga min petu Formosa Foundation te chungah lawmthu ka sawi a ni, a ti.

Pu Jonathan Lalremruata, Chairman Formosa Foundation pawhin thu a sawi a. Formosa Foundation chu tanpui ngaite tana ding, Mizoramin a mamawh an tih ve theih apiang ti thin a ni a. Oxygen concentrator pawh hi damloten tangkai takin lo hmang se kan duh a ni, a ti. Sorkar leh kan NGO te nena kan ram tana thawhho zel hi kan duh a ni, a ti bawk.

Oxygen Concentrator semna inkhawm hi Prof. Lalnuntluanga, Vice President, CYMA in a kaihruai a, Father Jerome Vungbiakdikan hunserh a hmang a. Prof. Malsawmliana, General Secretary, CYMA chuan report a pe a. Pu R. Lalngheta, President, CYMA pawhin thu a sawi bawk.

Tun tuma Oxygen Concentrator 500 te hi Mizoram chhung leh Assam, Tripura, Manipur- a YMA Branch thlan chhuahte bakah YLA leh MTP branch thenkhatten a hmunah hian an dawng nghal a. Zuangtui Branch YMA chuan an dawn tur hi Tripura Behliangchhip Branch te an kian a ni.

Attachment

title: Sawrkar chawlh May 28, 2026-ah sawn
content:
683/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 23rd May, 2026:Mizoram Sawrkar Holiday List ah  May 27, 2026 chu chawlh tia tarlan ni mah se, Id-Ul-Zuha (Bakrid) chu May 28, 2026-ah hman a nih dawn tak avangin, sawrkar chawlh chu May 28, 2026 (Ningani) ah sawn a ni tih vawiin khan GAD chuan thuchhuah a siam.

Department of Personnel, Govt. of India, Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, New Delhi-in ni 03.07.2025-a Office Memorandum a chhuah paragraph 5.2 angin, competent authority chuan Id-Ul-Zuha (Bakrid) Holiday chu 27 May 2026 (Nilaini) atangin 28 May 2026 (Ningani) — Zil Hajj thla ni 10-na — ah sawn a phalsak a ni.

Hemi ni-ah hian Sawrkar Office zawng zawng, Public Sector Undertakings, Educational Institutions leh Banking Institutions te chu khar vek a ni ang.

OM- Shifting of Id-ul-Zuha to 28th May 2028.pdf

Attachment
